growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 0.5 g/L caprolactam as sole C/energy source

up.method: growth on CAP as sole C source; no time-resolved substrate consumption measured at this concentration (only at 1.0 g/L)

yields.Yxs: DCW 0.48 g/L final biomass from 0.5 g/L CAP (Yxs~0.96 g DCW/g CAP if DCW is g/L; units in table printed as g/mL, treated as probable typo)

growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max from OD590 growth curves); td calculated td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.143=4.85 h
growth_records.snippet: 0.5 | lag 1.0 h | mu_max 0.143 h-1 | OD590 0.81 | DCW 0.48 +/- 0.03 (Table 2, Growth values of B. epidermidis BS3 vs. caprolactam concentrations)
growth_records.notes: Table 2 row 0.5 g/L CAP. mu_max=0.143 h-1 reported. td=ln2/0.143=4.85 h. Lowest CAP supporting growth (no growth at 0.4 g/L: mu=0, OD 0.03). DCW column header printed 'g/mL' but values (0.48) are only sensible as g/L; flagged as probable unit typo. Culture growth by OD590 conversion to DCW via calibration curve.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 1.0 g/L caprolactam as sole C/energy source (optimal)

up.method: GC-measured substrate depletion; 91.8% of 1.0 g/L CAP consumed over 40-120 h. 1.0 g/L / 113.16 g/mol = 8.837 mmol/L; 0.918*8.837=8.11 mmol/L over 80 h = 0.101 mmol/L/h
derived: volumetric 0.101 mmol/L/h divided by final DCW 0.98 g/L => 0.103 mmol/gDW/h; rough because biomass rises through the window

yields.Yxs: 0.98 g DCW / g CAP (final DCW 0.98 g/L from 1.0 g/L CAP; DCW header printed g/mL, treated as g/L typo)

growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max from OD590 growth curves); td calculated td=ln2/0.236=2.94 h
growth_records.snippet: 1.0 g/L: lag 6.0 h, mu_max 0.236 h-1, OD590 1.57, DCW 0.98 +/- 0.09 (Table 2). 'strain totally utilized this amount of CAP over 160 h... over 40-120 h decreased by 91.8%'
growth_records.notes: Optimal condition. mu_max=0.236 h-1 (Table 2). td=ln2/0.236=2.94 h. CAP uptake computed from Section 3.5 degradation experiment (same medium/temp/pH/1.0 g/L): CAP MW=113.16 g/mol (C6H11NO), 1.0 g/L=8.837 mmol/L; 91.8% consumed in 40-120 h (80 h) = 8.11 mmol/L /80 h = 0.101 mmol/L/h volumetric; specific ~0.103 mmol/gDW/h using DCW 0.98 g/L. Additional 5.2% consumed 120-160 h. No secreted products quantified (mineralization to CO2 implied via 6-AHA->adipic acid->beta-oxidation, not measured). DCW units in Table 2 printed 'g/mL', treated as probable g/L typo.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 2.0 g/L caprolactam as sole C/energy source

up.method: growth on CAP as sole C source; time-resolved consumption not measured at this concentration

yields.Yxs: DCW 0.92 g/L from 2.0 g/L CAP => ~0.46 g DCW/g CAP (DCW header g/mL treated as g/L typo)

growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max from OD590 curves); td=ln2/0.224=3.09 h calculated
growth_records.notes: Table 2 row 2.0 g/L CAP. mu_max=0.224 h-1 reported. td=ln2/0.224=3.09 h. Considered co-optimal with 1.0 g/L.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 5.0 g/L caprolactam as sole C/energy source
growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 row 5.0 g/L CAP. mu_max=0.146 h-1. td=ln2/0.146=4.75 h. Substrate inhibition apparent above optimum.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 10.0 g/L caprolactam as sole C/energy source
growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 row 10.0 g/L CAP. mu_max=0.080 h-1 (dramatic drop above this concentration). td=ln2/0.080=8.66 h.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 15.0 g/L caprolactam as sole C/energy source
growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 row 15.0 g/L CAP. mu_max=0.0543 h-1. td=ln2/0.0543=12.76 h.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 20.0 g/L caprolactam as sole C/energy source
growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.notes: Table 2 row 20.0 g/L CAP. mu_max=0.041 h-1. td=ln2/0.041=16.91 h.

growth_records.medium: Mineral salt medium (MSM) + 22.0 g/L caprolactam (critical max tolerated) as sole C/energy source
growth_records.cite: Esikova, Tatiana Z. et al. Epsilon-Caprolactam- and Nylon Oligomer-Degrading Bacterium Brevibacterium epidermidis BS3: Characterization and Potential Use in Bioremediation. Microorganisms (2023). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms11020373. [PMC9966071]
growth_records.snippet: 22.0 | lag 60 h | mu_max 0.022 h-1 | OD590 0.15 | DCW 0.08 +/- 0.001 (Table 2). Critical CAP at which growth still observed; no growth at 23.0 g/L.
growth_records.notes: Table 2 row 22.0 g/L CAP - critical maximum tolerance (highest reported among CAP degraders). mu_max=0.022 h-1, lag >60 h. td=ln2/0.022=31.51 h. No growth at 23.0 g/L or in substrate-free/inoculum-free controls. Strain also grows on 6-aminohexanoic acid, adipic acid, and 6-AHA linear dimer/trimer at 1.0 g/L (Fig 5) but no numeric growth rates/uptake given for those substrates, so no separate records emitted.
